programmes.show_description: Round-up of the latest headlines

programmes.show_name: It'll Be Alright on the Night
programmes.show_description: Compilation of TV clangers, gaffes and fluffed cues featuring Kylie Minogue, Michael McIntyre, King Charles III, Romesh Ranganathan, Sue Perkins, Roman Kemp and more. Plus, the moment Mary Berry almost burns down Alan Carr's house. Tom Allen hosts
Action from the latest matches, including Blackburn Rovers v Middlesbrough at Ewood Park, Ipswich Town v Millwall at Portman Road, and Swansea City v Coventry City at Swansea.com Stadium

programmes.show_name: Devil in Disguise: John Wayne Gacy
programmes.show_description: Kunkle and Sam are knee-deep in Gacy's trial as Tovar volunteers to drive Gacy to and from court, while attempting to compel him to come clean about how many people he killed, the disturbing tale of how Gacy befriended and groomed David Cram is revealed
Daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection

programmes.show_name: Julia Bradbury's Wonders of the Frozen South
programmes.show_description: Julia sails to the rugged island of South Georgia, which rises from the South Atlantic and has an extraordinary abundance of wildlife and a haunting human history. Landing on Salisbury Plain, Julia navigates to reach a vast colony of more than 100,000 king penguins that crowd the beach, as well as the whaling station that became polar explorer Ernest Shackleton's salvation, and sees some of the largest seals in the world
James welcomes singer Alfie Boe and Loose Women presenter Penny Lancaster to his Hampshire home, while also sampling recipes from fellow chefs Paul Rankin and Shivi Ramoutar

Jimmy Doherty and Shivi Ramoutar are joined in the kitchen by broadcaster Kaye Adams, who enjoys nasi goreng for breakfast and smacked cucumber and dumpling salad. Phillippa Gunn gives a lesson on creating easy colourful landscape paintings, Shivi learns how to navigate using nature with bushcraft and survival expert Mark Beckham and Jimmy gets up close and personal with the wildlife park's foxes
A round-up of the latest headlines

programmes.show_name: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programmes.show_description: Alan is joined at Manor Farm by actress Sarah Parish and singer Peter Andre. He also catches up with the zebras currently residing in Warwickshire, while a couple of llamas make quite an impression. Plus, advice on reviving houseplants after the winter months

The Fletchers throw open the farm gates and welcome families in to enjoy their annual festive event. Marnie and Milo don elf costumes to entertain the guests, and behind the scenes Liz and Kelvin work hard to make sure everyone enjoys a magical experience. Alongside the food and drink on offer, the first Fletcher beef is on sale and is proving popular. But the livestock still needs their attention too, as young tup Kelvin is limping, while tragedy hits the farm when a cow is stuck down by pneumonia
A weekly round-up of royal news, with expert analysis from ITV News' royal editor Chris Ship, expert Lizzie Robinson and their guests
Round-up of the latest headlines

The auditions make their way to Birmingham's Hippodrome Theatre, where guest judge Stacey Solomon - who knows a thing or two about auditioning in front of a live audience, after coming third on The X Factor in 2009 - joins the panel alongside KSI, Alesha Dixon and Amanda Holden

Prank show in which Joel Dommett, Judi Love, Sam Thompson and GK Barry work together to deceive unsuspecting members of the public by making them believe they are participating in new ITV shows. The first set of unsuspecting 'contestants' think they are taking part in business competition The Applicant, hosted by Dragons' Den star Sara Davies - who is in on the joke - while Jo Brand joins in the fun from Mission HQ as the rebellious guest prankster

programmes.show_name: Carabao Cup: Arsenal v Man City
programmes.show_description: Arsenal v Manchester City (Kick-off 4.30pm). Coverage of the final from Wembley Stadium, as the title rivals meet in the capital trying to claim the first major piece of silverware this season. City easily brushed Newcastle aside in the semi-finals thanks to a 5-1 aggregate victory, and Arsenal beat London rivals Chelsea 4-2 in their two-legged tie. Pep Guardiola's team have dominated this competition in recent years, winning six of the last 12 finals since 2014, while the Gunners haven't won this cup since 1993
Round-up of the latest headlines
Two more of the most dramatic endgames from the arcade-themed quiz show hosted by Ben Shephard, in which the last contestant standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programmes.show_name: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programmes.show_description: Another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with the four lifelines Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend, 50-50 and Ask the Host available to them if they get stuck. Jeremy Clarkson hosts

Michael is increasingly isolated as the investigation broadens and Annie doggedly pursues a new line of inquiry leading to the emergence of a compelling new suspect. Dylan reaches out to his headmaster for help in a desperate moment, Alana is rocked with indecision and Annie dares to let her guard down further with crushing consequences. As Dylan's frustrations rise and Alana takes a daunting leap, a devastating act rocks the investigation. Psychological thriller, starring Eve Myles and David Morrissey
Round-up of the latest headlines

programmes.show_name: In Our Blood: The Forever Chemicals Scandal
programmes.show_description: Documentary about the small North Yorkshire town of Bentham, which two years ago was recorded as the most contaminated place in the UK for PFAS - toxic so-called 'forever chemicals', some of which are linked to cancers and other serious illnesses. The film reveals the likely source of the pollution - a local factory that until 2025 manufactured firefighting foam - and follows residents and former workers, some battling cancer, as they undergo blood tests to find out whether the pollution in the environment has entered their bodies

Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell present the stories of two women searching for siblings. Tracey Boyle had children of her own before she learned of her brother's existence when her mother finally confided in her that her first-born son was prised from her hands at the age of 16. Liz Allward was adopted as a baby but always had a feeling that she had a biological sister and has longed to meet her since being proved right

Graham Norton hosts the game show as curriculum and quality manager Tana, train conductor Mikey and operations manager Charlott compete to solve word puzzles to get the chance to spin the wheel and win big money prizes, with a potential jackpot of £50,000
Daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection

The Fletchers throw open the farm gates and welcome families in to enjoy their annual festive event. Marnie and Milo don elf costumes to entertain the guests, and behind the scenes Liz and Kelvin work hard to make sure everyone enjoys a magical experience. Alongside the food and drink on offer, the first Fletcher beef is on sale and is proving popular. But the livestock still needs their attention too, as young tup Kelvin is limping, while tragedy hits the farm when a cow is stuck down by pneumonia
Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features
A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions
The key headlines from the day so far

Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity special of the classic game show, with contestants Joe Lycett, Chizzy Akudolu and Nick Knowles all trying to say what they see, as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen charity

Adil Ray hosts the game in which three pairs of contestants use their skills to find words from the fewest letters possible. The last remaining duo have the opportunity to potentially double the money they won earlier or leave with nothing. Taking part alongside the reigning champions are husband and wife Sue and Wayne, and cousins Serena and Neha
Stephen Mulhern hosts the exciting and iconic gameshow as another of his brave contestants takes on the infamous Banker for a chance to win a life-changing cash prize.

Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

Bradley Walsh hosts the show where thousands of pounds are at stake. All that stands between Matt from Kent, Serena from Derbyshire, Steve from Manchester, Nadia from Cardiff and that money is the small matter of one of the country's finest quiz brains
A round-up of the day's news headlines and talking points from the UK and around the world

The lambing season is full of challenges this year as Kelvin goes through a devastating loss when a three-day-old lamb breaks its leg, while the pigs go to auction, but will Kelvin make a return on his investment with prices at an all-time low? Elsewhere, Liz tries to get hands-on with the cows and the twins head out to explore blossoming bluebells as the season changes
Joe blackmails Robert and Aaron, Kim hires Ross, and Kerry is gobsmacked

Theo ensures that Todd misses his marathon, Will refuses to join his family in condemning Megan, Adam suffers a dramatic loss of business, and Bernie and Dev find Mal waiting upon their return from holiday

Alana, Annie and the rest of the police are reeling as Michael, who is numb with shock, is left to reckon with the enormity of what has happened. Ivan and Annie are at loggerheads as they try to grasp the meaning of the evidence before them, while the emergence of a shocking new suspect leads to a shattering conclusion where Annie's instincts and courage prove decisive. Psychological thriller, starring Eve Myles and David Morrissey
A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents
Current affairs show tackling the issues behind the headlines from a Scottish perspective, with studio debate and analysis of major stories
